title: Room-temperature intrinsic ferromagnetism in epitaxial CrTe2 ultrathin films

paper_analysis.summary: This paper reports epitaxial growth of stoichiometric mono- to few-layer CrTe2 on bilayer graphene by MBE and demonstrates intrinsic ferromagnetism persisting up to room temperature, with strong perpendicular magnetic anisotropy and a spin-split metallic band structure. The magnetic order is supported by SQUID, XMCD, and ARPES, and the monolayer still shows ferromagnetism with TC around 200 K, making ultrathin CrTe2 a promising 2D spintronic material.
measurement_types: Scanning tunneling microscopy (STM) topography and atomic-resolution imaging
X-ray diffraction (XRD) 2θ-ω scan and Laue fringes

figures.description: Schematic of MBE growth of CrTe2 films on graphene.
STM topography of a 7 ML CrTe2 film showing atomically flat terraces; inset optical image of a large-area film.
Line-scan profile across STM terraces showing uniform step height of about 6.14 Å.
XRD 2θ-ω diffraction pattern with Laue fringes around the CrTe2 (001) reflection and fitted thickness/roughness parameters.
Atomically resolved STM image showing hexagonal lattice structure.
Line profile from atomic-resolution STM image giving lattice periodicity of about 3.81 Å.
Temperature-dependent magnetization curves for CrTe2 films of different thicknesses under out-of-plane field.
Out-of-plane magnetic hysteresis loops of 7 ML CrTe2 at different temperatures.
In-plane magnetic hysteresis loops of 7 ML CrTe2 at different temperatures.
Expanded hysteresis loops at 300 K and inset temperature dependence of perpendicular anisotropy constant Ku.
figures.data_type: magnetization hysteresis curve / derived parameter plot

figures.description: Schematic of XMCD measurement geometry.
XAS and XMCD spectra of 7 ML CrTe2 from 5 K to 300 K, including integrals at 5 K.
Enlarged Cr L3-edge XAS spectra at 200 K, 250 K, and 300 K for different X-ray helicities.
Temperature dependence of spin moment ms and orbital moment ml derived from XMCD sum rules.
XAS and XMCD spectra of 1 ML CrTe2 at various temperatures.
Enlarged XAS near the Cr L3 edge showing helicity-dependent differences for 1 ML CrTe2.
XMCD percentage versus temperature for 1 ML CrTe2.
Thickness-temperature phase diagram compiling TC values from XMCD and SQUID.
ARPES valence-band dispersion of 7 ML CrTe2 along M-Γ-K.
DFT-calculated spin-polarized band structure of 7 ML CrTe2.
ARPES near-Fermi-level dispersion measured with He Iα photons and MDC peak fits.
ARPES near-Fermi-level dispersion measured with He IIα photons and MDC peak fits.
Combined experimental and theoretical near-EF band structure comparison.
Thickness-dependent ARPES intensity maps from 1 ML to 15 ML CrTe2.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: The paper reports raw experimental measurements from STM scans, XRD scans, SQUID magnetization curves, XAS/XMCD spectra, and ARPES intensity maps. It also includes TEM/HAADF-STEM images. The methods specify instrument settings and acquisition conditions, such as in-situ STM, MoKα1 XRD, SQUID up to 7 T, TEY-mode XAS/XMCD at beamline I10, and ARPES with He Iα/He IIα photons.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The authors state that the main data supporting the findings are available within the article and its Supplementary Information files, and that extra data are available from the corresponding authors upon reasonable request.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw measurement files are not explicitly described as deposited in a repository. The paper names the instruments and modalities used to collect data, but does not specify downloadable raw file formats or an open raw-data archive.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed/derived quantities include fitted XRD thickness and roughness, lattice constants from STM line profiles, Curie temperatures from M-T or XMCD fits, perpendicular anisotropy constant Ku, and spin/orbital moments derived from XMCD sum rules. ARPES peak positions are extracted by MDC fitting and compared with DFT bands.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-specific data are clearly presented in the article and Supplementary Information as plotted curves, spectra, images, and derived quantities. The paper does not explicitly say that source-data files for each figure are deposited separately.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repositories are mentioned in the paper text. DFT calculations are described methodologically, but no scripts are provided.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset is primarily an experimental characterization set plus theoretical DFT outputs. The paper emphasizes figure-ready processed data rather than a formal public raw-data repository. Supplementary Information is referenced for additional figures and supporting measurements. The main semantic distinction is: raw instrument outputs were collected, but the paper only explicitly guarantees availability of the main data in the article/SI and extra data on request.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The methods describe direct acquisition of STM, XRD, SQUID, XAS/XMCD, ARPES, and TEM measurements, implying raw instrument data were collected.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The paper presents many figure-ready curves and derived values, such as fitted XRD parameters, XMCD-derived moments, TC values, Ku, and MDC-extracted band positions.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: "The authors declare that the main data supporting the findings of this study are available within the article and its Supplementary Information files. Extra data are available from the corresponding authors upon reasonable request."
